title: Log Roll
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Patient with suspected spinal injuries.

#### Procedure

- Prepare any lifting devices (spine board, support straps, scoop stretcher or carry sheet).

- Remove items from pockets etc that may cause patient pain or pressure.

- Instruct assistants on procedure: 
- Brief assistant/s to stand/kneel on one side of patient and place hands on patient’s far side shoulder, hip, upper thigh and lower leg.

- Maintain neutral alignment of spine and support head.

- The person at the head end controls the process.

- Maintain neutral alignment of spine and support head.

- On pre-agreed count, roll patient in alignment toward assistants.

- Complete a secondary survey of patients back: 
- Check back pockets.

- If lifting device is required, instruct assistant to place device against patient’s back.

- Roll to supine position keeping head in alignment.

#### Complications

- Exacerbation of spinal injury if alignment is not maintained. Airway takes precedence over spinal injury.
- Spine boards/scoops are cold, hard and uncomfortable. They should be removed from underneath the patient as soon as practicable.
